input.background: A citizen claims that the intelligence of children in a particular community is above average. To test this claim, he selects a simple random sample consisting of n=9 children from this population. Scores of the intelligence test typically vary according to a Normal distribution with mean μ=100 and standard deviation σ=15. The sample produces these scores {116,128,125,119,89,99,105,116,118}. Thus, x̄=(116+128+⋯+118)/9=112.8.
input.question: Significance.

output.answer: The two-sided P-value provides significant evidence against the null hypothesis (reject H0 at α=0.05 but not at α=0.01).

sample_size.description: The number of children selected in the sample.

sample_scores.description: The intelligence test scores of the children in the sample.

population_mean.description: The mean intelligence test score in the general population.

population_standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of intelligence test scores in the general population.

sample_mean.description: The mean intelligence test score of the sample.
